text: The surgeon closes the colostomy and introduces the mobilized colon into the abdominal cavity. A 12mm Husson balloon trocar is implanted into the previous colostomy opening. Diagnostic laparoscopy reveals no further Crohn's disease lesions, and a segment of terminal ileum is identified for diverting loop ileostomy. The colonic stump is further mobilized from its attachments, evaluated for extension into the pelvis, and protruded after releasing the peritoneum.

The surgeon seals the marginal artery and applies a purse string suture clamp to remove the segment of colon leading to the end colostomy. The anvil of a 31mm circular stapler is implanted into the colonic stem and secured with the suture. The colon is reintroduced into the abdominal cavity, and a 12mm Hassan Ballantyre card is reimplanted. The rectal stump is pierced by the circular stapler shaft, which is dilated from 25mm to 33mm using upsizers. An end-to-end colorectal anastomosis is created, followed by a leak test.

The intraoperative flexible sigmoidoscopy reveals an intact colorectal anastomosis. The camera is adjusted to fit a five millimeter trocar, and the segment of terminal ileum is protruded through the colostomy site for the placement of a diverting loop ileostomy.
